_doc: Maps Job Club's 9 categories to the most likely Modern Award (by ID). Used by eligibility.py to look up the relevant minimum hourly rate from awards.json. The mapping is 'best fit' — many roles legitimately fall under multiple awards. The eligibility module compares pay against the chosen award's minimum and surfaces a note when there's significant ambiguity (esp. farm pieceworkers).

_award_names.MA000020: Building and Construction General On-site Award 2020

_notes.farm_piecework: Horticulture jobs paid by piece rate (per kg, per bin) have a separate piecework rate floor under MA000028. compare_pay() must skip strict comparison and surface a 'piece rate — verify manually' note when pay_kind=='piece'.
_notes.events_uses_hospitality: Most one-off event work (catering, function staff) is covered by Hospitality MA000009. Pure event-management contractor roles may not be — but those rarely target backpackers anyway.
_notes.animals_pastoral_choice: Stations and farms with livestock fall under Pastoral MA000035. Pet groomer / kennel hand technically fall under Animal Care & Veterinary Services MA000118, but these roles are rare in our data; defaulting to Pastoral covers the 88-day-relevant cases (cattle stations).
_notes.construction_award_caveat: MA000020 covers on-site construction. Joinery, plumbing services, electrical contracting have separate awards — but for a backpacker labourer the on-site award is typically the floor.
